112	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
113	  \label{thm:some_label}
114	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
115	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}

117	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A morphism $f: X \to Y$ of schemes locally of finite
118	  % type is said to be smooth at $x \in X$ if there exist an open affine

120	  % $U = \Spec A$ of $x$ with $f(U) \subset V$ such that ..."

136	
137	### Citation discipline (the hard rule)
138	
139	**Every declaration block that derives from external reference material requires three citation elements:**
140	
141	1. **`% SOURCE:` LaTeX comment** — pointer + local file. Format: `% SOURCE: <citation pointer> (read from references/<file>.md)`. The pointer is the source identifier + section/theorem/definition number + page when available (e.g. `[Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174` or `[Stacks Project], Tag 01V4`). The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical names the local file you opened to produce the verbatim quote.
142	2. **`% SOURCE QUOTE:` LaTeX comment** — the **verbatim text** of the cited statement, copied character-by-character from the local reference file you just named in the `% SOURCE:` line. Verbatim means:
143	   - **Original language.** French for Bourbaki / EGA, German for Grothendieck's early notes, English for Hartshorne / Vakil / Stacks, etc. Do NOT translate.

145	   - **Every word, every symbol.** No paraphrase, no "for brevity" abbreviation. The quote is the anti-hallucination signal — a writer who actually opened the source can paste; a writer reconstructing from memory cannot reproduce the source's exact words.
146	3. **Visible `\textit{Source: <pointer>.}` line** — first line of the prose body. Renders in the PDF so a mathematician reading the typeset blueprint sees the citation at a glance.
147

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d,
    lem:mapHC_augment_iso, lem:map_augment_cond, lem:augmentCochainIso}
  The augmentation node of both complexes is \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\), matched by the
  identity. At {\v C}ech degree \(p\) the object isomorphism between
  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p)\) and \(\prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma
  \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}. It remains to check the
  degreewise isomorphisms commute with the differentials.

  \emph{Augmentation bookkeeping (peeling the node).} Before matching differentials we strip the
  augmentation node off \(D^\bullet\). Writing \(\Psi = \operatorname{forget} \cdot
  \operatorname{restrictScalars}(\mathrm{id})\) for the push--pull adapter and \(G_V =
  \operatorname{toPresheaf} \cdot \operatorname{ev}_{V}\) for the section functor, the evaluated
  complex is \(D^\bullet = (G_V \circ \Psi)\bigl((\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet)
  .\operatorname{augment}\,(\operatorname{cechAugmentation})\bigr)\). Applying
  Lemma~\ref{lem:mapHC_augment_iso} twice --- once for \(\Psi\) and once for \(G_V\), each an
  additive functor, with the augmentation condition transported by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:map_augment_cond} --- commutes both functors past
  \(\operatorname{augment}\), so \(D^\bullet \cong \bigl((G_V \circ \Psi)
  \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet\bigr).\operatorname{augment}\,\bigl((G_V \circ \Psi)
  (\operatorname{cechAugmentation})\bigr)\) with identity components. Lemma~\ref{lem:augmentCochainIso}
  then glues an iso of augmented complexes from three pieces: the \emph{non-augmented} core iso
  \[
    \mathrm{coreIso} : (G_V \circ \Psi)\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
      \mathcal{F})\bigr) \;\cong\; \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}', \mathcal{F}),
  \]
  the augmentation-object iso \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F}) \cong \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) (the
  identity \(e_Y\)), and the degree-\(0\) compatibility square \(h_{\mathrm{compat}}\). This
  reduces the goal to the non-augmented \(\mathrm{coreIso}\), and the differential-match steps
  below apply to that reduced (non-augmented) goal --- not to the full augmented \(D^\bullet\),
  whose augmentation bookkeeping the three helper lemmas already discharge.

\emph{Augmentation differential.} The lowest differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the evaluation
  at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\)
  (Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}); read through the degree-\(0\) instance of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso} it is exactly the restriction product map
  \(t \mapsto (t|_{U_i \cap V})_i\), which is the augmentation map \(\varepsilon\) of
  \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) by construction. Both augmented complexes are well-formed by the
  shared identity \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}).
  Hence the degreewise maps assemble to an isomorphism of cochain complexes
  \(D^\bullet \cong D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\).

\emph{Augmentation differential.} The lowest differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the evaluation

  (Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}); read through the degree-\(0\) instance of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso} it is exactly the restriction product map
  \(t \mapsto (t|_{U_i \cap V})_i\), which is the augmentation map \(\varepsilon\) of
  \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) by construction. The compatibility square \(h_{\mathrm{compat}}\)
  fed to Lemma~\ref{lem:augmentCochainIso} is \emph{definitional up to the section-functor
  adapter}: once the adapter path \(\Psi = \operatorname{forget} \cdot
  \operatorname{restrictScalars}(\mathrm{id})\) followed by \(G_V = \operatorname{toPresheaf}
  \cdot \operatorname{ev}_{V}\) is unfolded, \(G_V(\Psi(\operatorname{cechAugmentation}))\)
  \emph{is} the restriction product \(\varepsilon\) on the nose, so the square closes by the
  identity / definitional reduction rather than a diagram chase --- it is precisely the
  degree-\(0\) instance of the differential match carried by \(\mathrm{coreIso}\). Both augmented
  complexes are well-formed by the shared identity \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}). Hence the degreewise maps assemble to an
